title: podman run hang with error: container creation timeout
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`podman run --rm -it --network=host alpine:3.14.3` hang and exit after a while with error:  `Error: container creation timeout: internal libpod error`

From the debug log, seems container has been successfully created with crun/runc, but podman failed to connect to the container somehow.

comments.body:
Looks like something is going wrong with the btrfs driver.  Could you switch to the overlay driver and see if this works.
btrfs driver is not well tested, and most of the upstream does not use it.

My read on this is that it looks like a conmon or crun issue - Conmon is not reporting back to Podman that the container was successfully created without our (very generous - I think it's 900 seconds?) timeout. @giuseppe Any way to get more verbose debug logs out of crun?

my suggestion is to see what processes are still running after a couple of minutes and use `strace` to see where they got stuck

Any reason for using the btrfs backend instead of overlay?

after switching to overlayfs the hang issue still persist:

I am experiencing what sounds like identical issue (rootless only) and I was able to track down additional details that perhaps can help:

1. When runc is asked to create container., the execution spec (config.json) passed to it lacks entry for the "user" namespace. It also lacks uid and gid mapping sections resulting in runc refusing to run rootless.  

Adding PODMAN_USERNS=auto env variable, forces the relevant sections to be created by code in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/container_internal_linux.go#L649.

Change in behavior (podman used to work before last upgrade) seems to have originated with https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/221b1add74e17ded10e8f2f832a53065578aa264# though I am not 100% sure. 

2. Conmon does not seem to be able to handle runc quitting with error message when TTY is enabled - I see that runc processes are in Zombie state while conmon is stuck in gmain loop forever  until I forcefully kill it.



The way I was able to identify figure out why runc was unhappy was by creating  below script and using it --runtime parameter to "podman run" so I could see how runc was requested to be executed and run it via CLI with additional debug flags instead without conmon in the middle.


`#!/bin/bash

LOGFILE="/tmp/runc.log-$$-$(date +%Y%m%dT%H%M%S)"
echo "Invoked with: $@" > $LOGFILE
sleep 600000
`
I will share more as I get a chance to debug

The flow is as follows:

1. Execute `/usr/bin/podman run -ti debian` in konsole 
2. conmon gets started by podman
3. crun gets started by conmon with "create" command
4. crun halts @ exec.fifo select @ https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/0.17/src/libcrun/container.c#L1035 

Reading code and https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/blob/main/runtime.md it seems another invocation of  crun with "start" command is what is expected to let container entry point actually execute but I am unable to find code path that is supposed to trigger that command.
